name: The Reverberation Ensemble VS The Library | Library of Ruina
description:
"…Let the performance begin. This will be the overture to us and the change we’re about to usher to the world."

_____________________________

A few warnings:

This bot has HEAVY spoilers for Library of Ruina, especially the late game, to avoid spoilers, LEAVE THIS INSTANT!

I heavily reccomend to use a proxy, as this is a pretty big bot regarding the tokens. The bot might confuse characters and appearances, so please don't get confused if that happens, either reroll or edit the message to your preferance. Now, I'd say I used Deepseek proxy like always, but seeing that it is going to be fully paid, I decided to find an alternative for my free proxy users, and Gemini seemed quite good! I'd say it remembers context x10 times better than Deepseek for this bot (tested with both, I liked Gemini more)

This takes place during the late game of Library of Ruina, the reception of "The Reverberation Ensemble".

ANY!POV | ({{user}} is NOT specified in the starter message!

IMPORTANT THINGS TO NOTE! READ THIS PART!

This bot is a full on ANY!POV, meaning you can not only use any persona, you can take control of the already existing characters.

To do so, you will need:

1 - A persona that matches the character. (For example, if you wanna RP as Roland, you will need a Roland persona made.)

2 - For your starting message, put this at the end of it:

(OOC: {{user}} takes role of (CHARACTER) in this scenario)

The bot should recognize it and let you control the actions over the character.

Since this bot gives you full on freedom to be whatever you want, you will 99% of the time have to specify who you are in your starter message, alternatively chat memory can work.

I'd also suggest handholding the bot with OOC commands at times if you don't get the desired result.

I noticed that on PC the starter message seems to cut off for some reasons? Probably Janitor being shocked... You might need to enlarge the screen to see sentences fully.

If you want the bot to fully remember things, use chat memory a lot (approximately each 3-4 messages)
